message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead">
You are the Parser Teammate on the claude-tracer development team. Your job is to implement the JSONL parser module.

**Project context:** claude-tracer is a trace visualization tool for Claude Code sessions. You are working in /Users/anhad/Projects/claude-tracer.

**Your assignment:** Task #1 - Implement the Parser module in src/parser/. 

**Key instructions:**
1. Read TaskGet for task #1 to get full details
2. Read the implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-02-18-claude-tracer-implementation.md (Phase 2, lines ~723-1185) for exact code and test specifications
3. Read the shared types in src/types/ to understand the interfaces you must implement
4. Follow TDD: write tests first in src/parser/__tests__/, verify they fail, then implement
5. You own ONLY src/parser/ - do not modify files outside this directory
6. Use @/types imports for shared type definitions
7. Commit each sub-task separately with descriptive messages
8. Push to remote when all sub-tasks are done
9. Mark task #1 as completed when done

**Sub-tasks:**
- Task 2.1: BaseParser abstract class (src/parser/base.ts)
- Task 2.2: ClaudeCodeParser for JSONL (src/parser/claude-code.ts)  
- Export everything from src/parser/index.ts

Run tests with: npx vitest run src/parser/
</teammate-message>

content.text: I'll start by reading my task details and the implementation plan.

content.content:
Task #1: Phase 2: Implement Parser module
Status: pending
Description: Implement the JSONL parser in src/parser/. Follow TDD - write tests first. Read the full implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-02-18-claude-tracer-implementation.md (Phase 2, starting around line 723). Tasks: (1) Create BaseParser abstract class in src/parser/base.ts with generateBlockId() and createSession() helpers. (2) Implement ClaudeCodeParser in src/parser/claude-code.ts that parses Claude Code JSONL format - handles user messages, assistant messages, tool_use, tool_result, thinking blocks. (3) Export from src/parser/index.ts. Use @/types imports for shared types. Commit each sub-task separately. Push when done.

task.subject: Phase 2: Implement Parser module
task.description: Implement the JSONL parser in src/parser/. Follow TDD - write tests first. Read the full implementation plan at docs/plans/2026-02-18-claude-tracer-implementation.md (Phase 2, starting around line 723). Tasks: (1) Create BaseParser abstract class in src/parser/base.ts with generateBlockId() and createSession() helpers. (2) Implement ClaudeCodeParser in src/parser/claude-code.ts that parses Claude Code JSONL format - handles user messages, assistant messages, tool_use, tool_result, thinking blocks. (3) Export from src/parser/index.ts. Use @/types imports for shared types. Commit each sub-task separately. Push when done.

content.text: Good, I have the full plan. Now let me read the shared types to understand the interfaces I need to implement.

content.text:
Tests fail as expected. Step 3: Create `src/parser/claude-code.ts`.

Note: The test for "parses tool result" requires the parser to track pending tool calls from a previous `parseLine` call (the "parses tool use" test). The tool_use_id `'tool-1'` is registered when parsing the tool_use line, then matched when parsing the tool_result. The tests run in order, and since they share the same `parser` instance (declared with `const parser = new ClaudeCodeParser()` at describe scope), state carries over between tests. Let me verify this logic is correct and implement accordingly.

content.content: File created successfully at: /Users/anhad/Projects/claude-tracer/src/parser/claude-code.ts

feat(parser): implement Claude Code JSONL parser

Implements ClaudeCodeParser that handles:
- User messages (plain text)
- Assistant messages (text content blocks)
- Tool use tracking (registers pending tool calls)
- Tool result matching (links results to pending calls)
- Thinking blocks
- Complete session parsing from multi-line JSONL

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
